brief: Review forge/ in this repo. CLI stack, UX, code quality, market-standards comparison as of May 11, 2026. Make it the best thing.
rationale: User asked for a review + 'make it the best'. Phase 4-6 would rewrite the forge CLI itself blindly. We stop after Council (Phase 3), surface must_fix and a redesign plan, and ask the user which parcels to schedule for Phase 4-5.

parcels.claim: Audit the runtime + dependency stack of forge CLI: Bun-only bin, hyparquet sole dep, no arg parser, manual spawnSync glue. Compare to May-2026 CLI norms (commander/cac/clipanion, @clack/prompts, citty/unjs).

UX audit: help text, error surfaces, interactive vs non-interactive modes, no progress spinners (raw spawnSync), no JSON output mode beyond doctor, emoji-heavy stdout, no --yes flag for confirmations, no --dry-run, no shell completion. Compare with Claude Code, Cursor CLI, aider, OpenCode, Codex CLI conventions.

Code-quality review of forge/src: 372-line install.ts mixing 7 concerns; spawnSync error paths swallowed; node_modules walk for hooks.json is O(n) recursive readdir; logLine reads-then-appends entire file each call; backup() never pruned; uninstall.ts duplicates state from install.ts; no shared types; doctor.ts has 11 inlined checks no abstraction.

Market-standard comparison: forge differentiates on 6-phase pipeline + signed audit chain + verify gates. Competing in May 2026: Claude Code (Anthropic baseline), aider (terminal-native), Cursor CLI, OpenCode (SST, plugin-based), Codex CLI (OpenAI), Gemini CLI, Continue, Aider-arch tools. What does forge uniquely own? What's table stakes it lacks (TUI, streaming output, MCP-first config, sandbox profiles, telemetry opt-out, cost dashboard)?

Design audit of the 6-phase pipeline itself: hardcoded mutation threshold 0.80 across all parcels (Stryker's own docs warn against universal cutoffs); council = 6 personas in parallel + meta-judge is expensive (~6x tokens) but mandatory even for typos; pbt-verify generates from a static property table — flawed for non-canonical fns; protect-mcp signing flow has fallback to npx @veritasacta/verify but real upstream signing is stubbed out (`regenerateProtectMcpHooks` writes empty hooks.json); audit-chain enforcement is theoretical when receipts are skill-generated not hook-signed.

Install/distribution flow: prereq installer auto-runs sudo (cargo, syft, grype, rustup install), assumes Linux/macOS, no Windows path, no idempotency check on already-installed tools beyond `command -v`, claude-mem patches marketplace.json by name-rewriting (brittle), 15 plugins/4 marketplaces all pinned to upstream HEAD (works against the project's stated 'latest-first' but no version pinning fallback).

Track 1 — audit-chain honesty (CRITICAL). README L82-area + SKILL.md Phase 6 claim 'protect-mcp signs every PreToolUse/PostToolUse → .forge/receipts/*.json'. Reality: forge's regenerateProtectMcpHooks() overwrites the protect-mcp hooks.json with {hooks:{}}, and signing is actually done gate-level by npx @veritasacta/verify sign-payload. Decision: TAKE PATH (b) — rewrite README + SKILL.md to honestly describe gate-level signing only; change regenerateProtectMcpHooks() to merge-not-overwrite so upstream signer's hooks stay alive if/when v2 ships. This matches meta-judge's recommendation ('do now regardless — documentation+10-line-hook fix') and avoids speculative Ed25519 hook implementation. Resolves: sfh-04 (critical), ca-04 (high).

Track 5 — types and shape (MEDIUM). Add ClaudeSettings interface to forge/src/types.ts (NEW FILE). Replace every Record<string, any/unknown> settings.json access in install.ts/doctor.ts/uninstall.ts/update.ts with the typed shape. Add template-literal PluginSpec type so plugin names like 'foo@marketplace-name' are validated against MARKETPLACES at compile time. Replace HealthResult plain-object union with discriminated union {kind:'ok'}|{kind:'warn',lines:string[]}|{kind:'fail',lines:string[]}. Delete the dead expression in state.ts logLine (await Bun.file(LOG_PATH).exists() ? null : null) and replace read+rewrite with appendFile (resolves sfh-05). Use crypto.randomUUID in writeJsonAtomic instead of process.pid (resolves cs-04). Resolves: tda-01, tda-03, tda-04, ca-03, sfh-05, cs-04.

Track 9 — doc cleanups (LOW). Every workaround comment in forge/src that flags a hack must link to the upstream issue (or be deleted if no longer applicable). Specifically: install.ts:161-162 (protect-mcp stub — link to obsolete evaluate/sign), install.ts:337-340 (claude-mem layout quirk — link to upstream packaging decision), install.ts:104/113/116/119/197-199/353-355 audited. Update cli.ts:1-6 stale docstring (says plugin lives at github.com/youssefKadaouiAbbassi/forge — actually monorepo at code-tools). Decision on claude-mem upstream PR: defer — the meta-judge says 'PR the claude-mem marketplace.json fix upstream' but code-reviewer cr-05 frames it as 'either upstream OR document as known fragile + add CI test'; we take the document path here since opening upstream PR is out-of-scope for this run. Resolves: ca-01, ca-02, cr-05.

Wire `tdd-guard-bun` reporter (or 30-line custom bun reporter) so Bun tests write valid JSON to `.claude/tdd-guard/data/test.json` matching the schema at `tdd-guard/dist/contracts/schemas/reporterSchemas.js`. Without a real reporter, tdd-guard falls back to LLM-judge mode which rejected the 3 deferred items 7+ times. Adds bun reporter config to `forge/bunfig.toml` (new file) or `forge/package.json` test script. Verifies by running `bun test tests/L0/types-shape.test.ts` and asserting `.claude/tdd-guard/data/test.json` exists with valid structure.

Follow-up to track 1 code half. `regenerateProtectMcpHooks()` at `forge/src/install.ts:165-199` currently overwrites every protect-mcp `hooks.json` it walks with `{hooks:{}}`, clobbering hypothetical upstream protect-mcp v2 (which would ship valid commands). Change behavior: read each candidate file, scan for `/protect-mcp\s+(evaluate|sign)\b/` (the obsolete subcommands), and ONLY clobber files matching that pattern. Files without the pattern are left untouched. Un-skip the pinned `L0.audit-1` test in `forge/tests/L0/types-shape.test.ts`. Resolves sfh-04 (critical) and ca-04 (high) — code half.

Follow-up to track 5. `forge/src/doctor.ts:75-80` declares a local `interface HealthResult` that shadows the canonical strict-discriminated-union from `forge/src/types.ts`. Delete the local interface, import the canonical one. Update `printResult` at line 82-86 to narrow `r.lines` via `"lines" in r` so the discriminated union compiles. Resolves tda-04 final migration.

Follow-up to track 5. `forge/src/state.ts:81-87` `logLine` reads-then-rewrites entire LOG_PATH on every call — O(n^2) growth and a lost-write race. The `appendFile` import is already at line 1 of state.ts. Replace `readFile + writeFile` with `await appendFile(LOG_PATH, line, "utf8")`. Remove now-unused `readFile, writeFile, existsSync` imports if no other consumer remains. Resolves sfh-05 final fix.
